name: AI Crypto Deep Research Benchmark
description: Public benchmark tasks for evaluating markdown-only crypto deep research answers.

tasks.prompt: Find crypto projects without a token that raised more than $10M since 2024. Verify token status, funding amount, and major investors.
expectations: List qualifying projects
Verify token status with explicit evidence
Include funding amount and lead investors
Acknowledge coverage limits if completeness is uncertain
red_flags: Treats rumor as confirmation
Claims token status without evidence
Presents an incomplete list as exhaustive

tasks.prompt: Research the Hyperliquid ecosystem: team background, token design, market structure advantages, recent partnerships, and major risks.
expectations: Cover team or founding context
Explain token design or incentive mechanics
Assess competitive advantages and risks
Use multiple source types
red_flags: Pure marketing language
No discussion of risks
No source diversity

tasks.prompt: Analyze the correlation between Bitcoin ETF inflows, on-chain whale accumulation, and BTC price action over the last 6 months. Explain what is signal and what is noise.
expectations: Cover all three variables
Separate correlation from causation
Discuss timing and lag effects
State uncertainty clearly
red_flags: Claims causation without support
Ignores conflicting evidence
Uses stale time framing

tasks.prompt: Compare the fee revenue models of Uniswap, Aave, and MakerDAO. Which looks most sustainable over the next market cycle, and why?
expectations: Explain each protocol's revenue model
Compare sustainability drivers and risks
Reference current or recent protocol context
Offer a defensible conclusion
red_flags: Shallow list with no comparison
No downside analysis
Unsupported winner declaration

tasks.prompt: Build a deep research brief on Bitcoin Layer 2s comparing Lightning, Stacks, RGB, and Citrea across adoption, developer activity, and product readiness.
expectations: Cover all four ecosystems
Compare adoption and development signals
Discuss product readiness and limitations
Avoid false equivalence when data quality differs
red_flags: Omits one of the requested projects
Pretends incomparable data is directly comparable
No caveats about immature ecosystems

tasks.prompt: Investigate whether there is alpha in following smart-money wallets that bought tokens before major 10x runs. Focus on timing, wallet type, token profile, and false positives.
expectations: Evaluate whether the hypothesis holds
Include false-positive scenarios
Differentiate wallet archetypes
Avoid overclaiming actionable alpha
red_flags: Cherry-picked examples
No false-positive analysis
Turns a weak pattern into a trading promise

tasks.prompt: Compare USDT, USDC, Ethena USDe, and Sky Dollar (USDS) as of 2026-03-17 across reserve design, redemption mechanics, yield source, depeg history, and regulatory risk. Which looks most resilient in a real stress event, and why?
expectations: Cover all four stablecoin systems
Explain reserve design, redemption path, and yield source for each
Discuss depeg history or stress-test failure modes
Offer a defensible resilience ranking with clear caveats
red_flags: Treats all dollar-pegged assets as equivalent
Ignores redemption mechanics or reserve composition
Makes a winner declaration without downside analysis

tasks.prompt: Analyze whether tokenized Treasury and RWA products are becoming real DeFi collateral rails or remain mostly parked yield as of 2026-03-17. Use Ondo, the BlackRock BUIDL and Securitize stack, Maple, and Centrifuge as case studies.
expectations: Use all requested case studies or clearly explain any substitution
Separate passive yield parking from actual collateral integration
Discuss liquidity, composability, and regulatory constraints
Reach a defensible conclusion on whether RWAs are becoming core rails
red_flags: Equates asset growth with DeFi composability
Ignores permissioning or redemption frictions
Uses TVL growth as sufficient proof of product-market fit

tasks.prompt: Find crypto tokens facing meaningful circulating-supply pressure from scheduled unlocks, treasury sales, or incentive emissions between 2026-04-01 and 2026-12-31. Separate headline unlock size from realistic liquid sell pressure.
expectations: Identify multiple tokens with material upcoming supply pressure
Distinguish scheduled unlocks, treasury sales, and incentive emissions
Explain why headline unlock size can differ from liquid sell pressure
State assumptions and uncertainty where schedules or treasury behavior are unclear
red_flags: Treats fully diluted supply and liquid float as the same thing
Uses stale unlock calendars or outdated token schedules
Assumes every unlocked token is immediately sold

tasks.prompt: Analyze whether perpetual futures trading is structurally migrating on-chain as of 2026-03-17. Compare Hyperliquid, dYdX, Jupiter, and major centralized exchanges on liquidity quality, product breadth, execution trust, and where CEXs still dominate.
expectations: Compare all requested venue types rather than just one chain or one exchange
Use market structure evidence such as volume quality, liquidity depth, and product breadth
Separate temporary trading spikes from durable share shifts
Explain where centralized venues still have structural advantages
red_flags: Uses one short time window as the whole story
Equates raw volume with durable market-share capture
Ignores execution quality or product coverage differences

tasks.prompt: Evaluate the crypto AI-agent token sector as of 2026-03-17. Which projects show real product usage, fee generation, or distribution, and which mostly trade on narrative beta? Use one consistent framework across the projects you select.
expectations: Define the sector and selection criteria clearly
Apply one consistent framework across multiple leading projects
Distinguish product usage, distribution, and revenue from token price action
Acknowledge data-quality limits and where evidence is weak
red_flags: Uses marketing copy instead of evidence of usage
Confuses social attention with product traction
Makes sector-wide claims without a clear comparison framework

tasks.prompt: Analyze whether corporate Bitcoin treasury strategies create durable shareholder value or mainly repackage leveraged BTC exposure as of 2026-03-17. Compare Strategy, Metaplanet, Semler Scientific, and at least one newer entrant on funding structure, dilution, downside reflexivity, and market perception.
expectations: Compare at least four public-company treasury strategies
Explain financing structure, dilution, and leverage mechanics
Discuss downside reflexivity and why market pricing may diverge from spot BTC exposure
Offer a balanced conclusion rather than a one-sided bull or bear case
red_flags: Treats treasury equities as simple spot BTC proxies
Ignores dilution, debt terms, or refinancing risk
Confuses market premium with durable business quality
